name: X/Twitter Automation
description: Battle-tested playbook for X/Twitter browser automation via CDP. Covers feed, like, reply, retweet, bookmark, create post, search, follow/unfollow, and profile viewing. X has excellent data-testid attributes for reliable selectors.

steps: Use browser_human_click on the Like button
Verify: [data-testid='unlike'] now exists in that tweet
guards: Tweet must not already be liked (no unlike testid)

steps: Wait 1.5s for reply dialog to open
Type reply via browser_fill_form with ~70ms delay
Use JS dispatch (mousedown + mouseup + click) on the retweet button
Wait 1s for retweet dropdown menu to appear
Verify: [data-testid='unretweet'] now exists in that tweet
guards: Tweet must not already be retweeted
retweet.why: browser_human_click does not reliably open the retweet menu on X. JS dispatch with full mousedown+mouseup+click sequence works.

steps: Use JS dispatch (mousedown + mouseup + click) on the bookmark button
Verify: [data-testid='removeBookmark'] now exists in that tweet
bookmark_tweet.why: Like retweet, bookmark requires JS dispatch events, not browser_human_click.

steps: Wait 1.5s for compose dialog to open
Type tweet via browser_fill_form with ~60ms delay
create_post.why: X has two post buttons: tweetButtonInline (in-page compose) and tweetButton (dialog compose). Try inline first.

steps: Wait 2s for search results to load
search.why: Direct URL navigation is more reliable than typing in search box + Enter. X's search input doesn't respond to JS keydown Enter events.

steps: Use JS dispatch (mousedown + mouseup + click) on Follow button
follow_profile.why: X follow buttons use data-testid format '{userId}-follow' where userId is numeric.

steps: Use JS dispatch click on Following button
Navigate to x.com/messages (may require DM passcode)
Click new chat button: [data-testid='dm-new-chat-button'] via JS dispatch
Wait 1.5s for new DM dialog to open
Type recipient username into input[data-testid='new-dm-search-input'] via browser_fill_form
Wait 2s for search results to load
Click matching recipient from li elements in dialog via browser_human_click
Wait for message composer to appear
Set message text in textarea[data-testid='dm-composer-textarea'] via native value setter + input event
Press Enter via keydown event to send
Verify: new message appears in [data-testid^='message-text-'] list
guards: DM passcode must be entered if enabled
send_dm.why: X DM textarea requires native value setter (Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptor(HTMLTextAreaElement.prototype, 'value').set) to bypass React. browser_fill_form and browser_type don't persist values. Enter keydown sends the message.

steps: Extract visible tweets: username from [data-testid='User-Name'], text from [data-testid='tweetText']
Scroll down to load more tweets

guards: Profile must not be private or suspended

errors.context: Any action when not logged in
errors.solution: Log in manually first. ScreenHand cannot bypass login.

errors.context: Too many likes, retweets, or replies
errors.solution: Wait 15-30 minutes. X rate limits are strict.

errors.error: Retweet/Bookmark menu not opening with browser_human_click
errors.context: CDP mouse events at element coordinates don't trigger X's React event handlers for dropdown menus
errors.solution: Use JS dispatch: el.dispatchEvent(new MouseEvent('mousedown', {bubbles:true})); el.dispatchEvent(new MouseEvent('mouseup', {bubbles:true})); el.dispatchEvent(new MouseEvent('click', {bubbles:true}));

errors.error: Share button triggers native share sheet
errors.context: X's share post button opens the OS-level share sheet, not a web dropdown
errors.solution: Cannot capture via CDP. Use Copy Link from the tweet's more options (caret) menu instead, or construct the URL manually: x.com/{username}/status/{tweetId}.

errors.error: Search doesn't submit on Enter key via JS
errors.context: JS keydown/keypress events for Enter don't trigger X's React search submission
errors.solution: Navigate directly to x.com/search?q={query}&src=typed_query instead of typing in search box.

errors.context: X DMs are protected by a 4-digit passcode on the recovery page
errors.solution: Use browser_fill_form to type digit '1' into first input[type='text'][placeholder=' '] — inputs auto-advance. Or navigate directly if passcode was already entered this session.

errors.error: DM textarea doesn't hold value from browser_fill_form or browser_type
errors.context: X DM composer textarea uses React controlled input that ignores CDP key events
errors.solution: Use native value setter: Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptor(HTMLTextAreaElement.prototype, 'value').set.call(textarea, text); then dispatch input event. Send with Enter keydown.

errors.error: New DM search result click doesn't select user
errors.context: Clicking li elements in new DM dialog doesn't always register
errors.solution: Use existing conversations via [data-testid*='dm-conversation-item'] with JS dispatch click as fallback.

errors.error: Tab IDs change after Chrome reconnect
errors.context: CDP tab IDs are regenerated when MCP server restarts
errors.solution: Always call browser_tabs first to get fresh tab IDs before interacting.

errors.error: Frontmost app switches to VS Code when approving tool calls
errors.solution: Use CDP browser tools (browser_js, browser_human_click, browser_fill_form) — they work regardless of which app is frontmost.

safety: Don't exceed rate limits — X suspends aggressively
Some actions need JS dispatch instead of browser_human_click (retweet, bookmark, follow)
Use browser_fill_form with 50-100ms delays for typing
tool_preferences: browser_human_click — for Like and Reply buttons
JS dispatch (mousedown+mouseup+click) — for Retweet, Bookmark, Follow (menus/state changes)
browser_fill_form — for tweet compose and reply textboxes
browser_navigate — for search (direct URL) and profile navigation
browser_js — for extracting data, clicking confirm dialogs, verifying state changes

steps.description: Wait for new tweets to load
